name: Hajjar ibn Abjar
title: Oath-Breaking Commander
shortDescription: One of Kufa's elders who wrote an invitation letter to Imam Hussain (AS), but came to fight him on Ashura with a thousand horsemen.
fullStory:
Hajjar ibn Abjar al-'Ijli was one of the most prominent tribal leaders in Kufa. Like Shabath ibn Rib'i, he was among those who wrote passionate letters inviting Imam Hussain (AS) to Kufa, claiming 'armies are ready at your service'.

However, when Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad became the governor of Kufa and adopted a policy of bribery and threats, Hajjar ibn Abjar sold his religion for the world and joined Yazid's army.

On the day of Ashura, he commanded a thousand horsemen and stood against the very person he had invited. Imam Hussain (AS) called his name and a few other commanders on Ashura, saying, 'Did you not write to me?' But he shamelessly denied it.

After the Ashura event, Hajjar remained in Kufa, but unlike many of Karbala's killers who were killed by Mukhtar, he managed to escape or evade punishment and lived for years with the stigma of breaking his covenant.
quoteArabic: يا حَجّارَ بْنَ أَبْجَرَ... أَلَمْ تَكْتُبُوا إِلَيَّ أَنْ قَدْ أَيْنَعَتِ الثِّمارُ...؟
quote: (Imam Hussain addressing him:) O Hajjar ibn Abjar! Was it not you who wrote that the fruits have ripened and we await you?
